description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 50-53.

Legendary Monk

There is a story told in certain monasteries–the kind built into cliffsides, accessible only to those who already know the way–about a student who wished to stop the rain. Though his fellows laughed at his goal, he stood beneath pouring drops for days and days, attempting to punch, kick, or otherwise detain something that seemed unstoppable. His master, moved by the student’s dedication, walked behind him, into the rain, and told him to simply hold out his hand. The raindrops broke against his skin, the water flowed through his fingers, and fell onto the ground. “You cannot yet end the storm, young one, but the rain cannot deter you. To the clouds, you are the unstoppable one.”

Seventy years later, the student would return to the monastery. When the rain came, he stood beneath the drops and, with a single punch, created a shockwave so powerful it pushed the clouds from the sky above onto neighboring lands. He remained unstoppable; the clouds did not.

A Legendary Monk has spent a lifetime in conversation with something most people ignore–the current of energy that runs beneath muscle and bone and breath, the fundamental force that connects a mortal to the living cosmos. All is one, and one is all. The clarity of meditation becomes understanding of the highest order: there is a flame within all of us–one that must be tended, if we dare to light it. The Monk moves through reality the way water breaks down a rock–not by force, but persistence. In a cosmos defined by the devastations of hubris, the Monk is the rare thing that does not need any of it, trusting in their own soul, diminutive though it is compared to the world, and achieving the greatest of powers through enlightenment.

At 20th level, the Monk has gone so far past the limits of the physical that they transcend what is possible. They consciously move faster than reflex. They strike with a precision that bypasses armor, resistance, and even magic. They walk on thin air and control every single fiber of their muscles. What defines them at this tier is perfection. A chaos of movement, yet in absolute control–the eye of something that, from the outside, looks like a terrible storm.

Resistance, Body Mind Soul (5e) 1 1d12 20 +30 ft.

Mythical Technique (1), Wellspring of Ki 1 1d12 21 +30 ft.

Legendary Monk

Bonus Features Legendary

Level Proficiency

20th +6 World Strike, Legendary AC (Monk), Legendary

21st +6 Heroic Path (1), Legendary Score Improvement (1),

22nd +7 Heroic Path (2), Ki-Borne Awareness, Masterful

Deflection 2 1d12 22 +30 ft.

23rd +7 Heroic Path (3), Legendary Score Improvement (2),

Suppleness of Wind & River, Reactive 2 1d12 23 +40 ft., fly 40 ft.

24th +8 Heroic Path (4), Focus Beacon, Improved World

Strike, Soulful Recovery, Mythical Technique (2) 3 1d12 24 +50 ft., fly 50 ft.

25th +8 Heroic Path (5), Legendary Score Improvement (3),

Power of the White Flame 3 1d12 30 +60 ft., fly 60 ft.

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Monk table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

Monk’s AC. Increase your AC by 2. This bonus is increased by 1 when you reach 22nd and 24th level.

World Strike

When you reach 20th level, you learn to channel your martial prowess into a singular, ki-augmented strike. You can use your action and expend 1 Legendary Point to make an unarmed attack or melee weapon attack against a creature within range. This attack deals 4d8 x

Focus Points

Martial

Unarmored

Arts

Movement

Points

5 Soul damage to that target. This attack does not benefit from any bonus to its damage. On a critical hit, you do not multiply the damage dice by 2, but instead deal 6d8 x 5 Soul damage. You cannot use your Stunning Strike on this attack.

On a hit, you can expend 8 Focus points to force the target to make a DC 30 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the target is stunned until the end of its next turn.

Body, Mind, Soul (5e)

5e. When you reach 20th level, increase your Dexterity and Wisdom scores by 4. Your maximum for these ability scores is increased to 25. In addition, you can expend 3 Ki points to bolster yourself against harm for 1 minute or until you have the Incapacitated condition. During that time, you have Resistance to all damage except Force damage.

Level 21

Heroic Path Advancement

At 21st level, you choose a Heroic Path you meet the prerequisites for and advance its first step. Each time you gain a level, you can advance a Heroic Path by one step, start a new Heroic Path, or, if specified as an option of the current step you’re on, jump from one Heroic Path into another.

Legendary Score Improvement

When you reach 21st level, and again at levels 23 and 25, increase one of your ability scores by 2 or two ability scores by 1. You cannot raise an ability score above 24 using this feature, unless another feature raised your maximum for that specific ability score beforehand.

Wellspring of Ki

Starting at 21st level, you learn to enter a state of profound wholeness that rejuvenates and invigorates your soul. You can enter this state as an action. For the next minute, you regain 3 Focus points at the start of each of your turns. Once you enter this state, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Mythical Technique

At 21st level, you learn a Mythical Technique from the Mythical Techniques section. You learn an additional Mythical Technique when you reach 24th level.

Level 22

Ki-Borne Awareness

At 22nd level, you learn to use your Focus to pierce through any illusion and detect those that are hidden. As a bonus action on your turn, you can expend 3 Focus points to gain truesight out to a range of 30 feet.

You can expend additional Focus points beyond the initial 3 to extend the range by 10 feet per additional Focus point expended as part of the same bonus action. This feature lasts for a minute or until you are incapacitated.

Masterful Deflection

At 22nd level, you learn to catch lightning, bend fire, and redirect even psychic force back at those who would harm you. When you take damage from a type you are resistant to, you can use your reaction and expend 1 Legendary Point to take no damage instead.

When you use this reaction, you can choose to redirect the mitigated force toward a creature you can see within 60 feet of you by expending 5 Focus points. If you do so, that creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take Soul damage equal to the damage mitigated, up to a maximum of 100. You can expend additional Focus points beyond the fifth to increase this maximum. For each additional Focus point you expend as part of this reaction, the maximum Soul damage the target can take increases by 20, with no upper limit on the number of Focus points you can spend this way.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

Level 23

Suppleness of Wind & River

Beginning at 23rd level, you learn to step on air itself. You gain a fly speed equal to the speed gained by your Unarmored Movement feature (see Legendary Monk

table). In addition, your Unarmored Movement is increased by 10 feet, and again by another 10 feet when you reach 24th and 25th level.

In addition, whenever you gain a Fly speed greater than the speed granted by this feature, you can add your Unarmored Movement bonus to the new fly speed.

Reactive

Also at 23rd level, your unparalleled dexterity allows you to act with greater reflexes. You can take one additional reaction per round. You cannot take the same reaction twice in a round, and can only use one reaction per triggering action.

Level 24

Focus Beacon

Starting at 24th level, the base use of your Ki-Borne Awareness feature does not expend Focus points. You must still expend Focus points to extend your truesight beyond 30 feet as a bonus action. This increase lasts for a minute or until you are incapacitated.

Improved World Strike

When you reach 24th level, your World Strike damage calculation is increased to 7d8 x 5, or 9d8 x 5 on a critical hit.

Soulful Recovery

At 24th level, when you use the Recover action, you regain 5 Focus points.

Level 25

Power of the White Flame

At 25th level, your soul expands and beams with the White Flame of mortalkind. Your Focus Points are increased to 30. Any feature or spell that would affect your soul, such as the Soul Strike Mythical Technique, automatically fails when targeting you, unless you choose to be affected. Your maximum hit points cannot be reduced in any way.

In addition, you learn to use your Focus to fuel your most powerful abilities. Instead of expending Legendary Points, you can choose to expend Focus points at a rate of 10 Focus points per Legendary Point.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
